text: Which bounded IPC primitive should cross the parent-to-exec-child boundary?
why_it_matters: The Charter requires IPC but does not choose a primitive; the answer fixes framing, inherited-descriptor cleanup, malformed-input handling, and later corpus reuse.
unlocks: CORP-FR-005, M0-CORPUS-IPC-v1, protocol negative tests, and exec-child cleanup evidence.

options.label: Unix seqpacket socketpair
options.pros: Bidirectional and message-bounded with straightforward malformed-frame tests.
options.cons: Linux-specific and requires careful close-on-exec handling.

options.label: Two pipes
options.cons: Requires stream framing and twice the descriptor cleanup.

options.label: Shared memory plus eventfd
options.cons: Adds mapping and lifetime complexity outside the M0 fixture need.

default: Use one AF_UNIX SOCK_SEQPACKET socketpair, intentionally duplicate only the child endpoint to descriptor 3 across exec, and use the versioned frames bounded to 256 bytes.

answer.text: Option 1: use one AF_UNIX SOCK_SEQPACKET socketpair; intentionally duplicate only the child endpoint to descriptor 3 across exec, use versioned frames bounded to 256 bytes, and fail closed on malformed framing or descriptor state.
